title: Dollhouse (280850)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Dollhouse
- Steam AppID of the game: 280850

**System Information**
- GPU: RX 5700 ITX
- GPU Driver: Mesa 20.1.2 - kisak-mesa PPA
- Kernel version: 5.8.0-050800-lowlatency
- Distro version: 19.3 Tricia (64 bit) (Cinnamon)
- Proton version: 5.0-9

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms 

Game is working well except that videos are not played

## Reproduction

- Start a new campaign
- Right at the beginning of chapter 1 when you first get control of your character there is a projector on the table that is supposed to project video on the wall.

Another for issue #1464, videos are: MP4, AVC+AAC
"mfplat.dll" is mentioned in the logs


I tried also the game with unofficial Proton-5.11-GE-1-MF, and with that videos worked except one I believe during the intro that was skipped.

[media_info.txt](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/4841034/media_info.txt)
[steam-280850.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/4841035/steam-280850.log)
[system_info.txt](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/4841036/system_info.txt)

title: Denuvo connection issue
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Sonic Frontiers
- Steam AppID of the game: 1237320

## System Information
- GPU: RX 5600 XT
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 22.3.1
- Kernel version: 6.1.1
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/DigitalDragon64/2509f5311e653a9dbf8c8910805df9d2
- Proton version: 7.0-5

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.


[steam-1237320.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/10333659/steam-1237320.log)

## Symptoms 
Game does not start. Instead I get a window with the message

'The server is not reachable. Check your Internet connection and click "Retry"
In case the problem persists, click "Next" to open the support website in the default browser'
![20230102_23h40m56s_grim](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10868601/210283388-71ec2076-41ee-49fc-99ab-3b5ce5544f0a.png)

The next window where I have manually to open the website doesn't show the code, so I can't proceed with the manual method.
![20230102_23h42m19s_grim](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10868601/210283389-26f39d68-76dd-4672-909a-def509b41b71.png)


## Reproduction
Starting the game

title: Derail Valley (588030)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Derail Valley (also happens with Onward and VR Trivia Battle)
- Steam AppID of the game: 588030

## System Information
- GPU: GTX 1080
- Driver/LLVM version: Nvidia 415.27
- Kernel version: 4.18.0-13-generic
- Link to full system information report as [Gist]: https://gist.github.com/FictionX/2442db849b89a45f378a9b2b1e5fdb6b#file-gistfile1-txt
- Proton version: 3.16-x

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.


Log too big to be added - even zipped.

## Symptoms 

Game crashes as soon as a controller (tested with Vive wands or xbox 360 controller) is turned on. If they're turned on before the game is started, it crashes as soon as the game checks for input.

This also happens for games Onward and VR Trivia Battle

Switching to Proton v 3.7-8, the games do not crash when the controllers are active (but instead they have other 3.7-related issues like missing objects).


## Reproduction

Start game, confirm that it responds to input from the HMD - and then turn on a controller. The game will crash. SteamVR and the Steam client remains unaffected.

title: Stormworks: Build and Rescue (573090)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Stormworks: Build and Rescue
- Steam AppID of the game: 573090

## System Information
- GPU: RX 5700 XT Anniversary Edition
- Driver/LLVM version: MESA 20.1.8, LLVM 10.0.1
- Kernel version: 5.8.13-1-MANJARO
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/CorvetteCole/e6b2dde09a4f63ba5541f8c1e49cc002
- Proton version: 5.13-1

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game. (there was one, but it is unrelated)
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

#### Proton log:
The file was 55.4MB, so I have uploaded it to Google Drive and included the link below. Sorry.
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1bn-A0Kf_f16q3Y59v2w9ew7U4-u7282I/view?usp=sharing

## Symptoms 
The game launches fine and appears to run fine, with everything in the menus being clickable and working properly. When you load in to a game, the 3D camera is not fine. Small mouse movements cause the camera to spazz out and jump around everywhere. This makes the game completely unplayable. It used to work alright, but I think some combination of Proton and system software updates broke it. I attempted to perform regression testing but could not make it work on older kernel versions etc either.


## Reproduction
Launch the game, start a new save or load an existing one, attempt to look around once loaded. It will not follow your mouse movements.
